name: Subnautica
description: Descend into the depths of an alien underwater world filled with wonder and peril. Craft equipment, pilot submarines and out-smart wildlife to explore lush coral reefs, volcanoes, cave systems, and more - all while trying to survive.
requirements:
Minimum:
  • Requires a 64-bit processor and operating system
  • OS *: Windows Vista SP2 or newer, 64-bit
  • Processor: Intel Haswell 2 cores / 4 threads @ 2.5Ghz or equivalent
  • Memory: 4 GB RAM
  • Graphics: Intel HD 4600 or equivalent - This includes most GPUs scoring greater than 950 points in the 3DMark Fire Strike benchmark
  • DirectX: Version 11
  • Storage: 20 GB available space
  • VR Support: SteamVR. Keyboard or gamepad required
comment1: Absolutely phenomenal, I've never had such an immersive experience in any other game.
comment2:
[b] Subnautica is a survival game on an alien ocean planet. It starts calm with exploration and resource gathering but quickly gets more intense the deeper you go. The world is immersive and the amazing music really adds to the atmosphere and tension. It can feel a bit slow at times but the exploration, mystery and overall vibe make it really worth playing. [/b]

comment3:
Gifted to two people this week.

Probably the best single player game experience I have ever enjoyed. And completed multiple times on hardcore, including runs with multiple prohibitions, such as no use of air tanks, rebreather, or bases, except where necessary to unlock subsequent recipes. 

Although, I failed my first 8 attempts at hardcore with no restrictions, cumulatively totalling 14 hours of losses.

You won't regret playing this game. My first playthrough was 78 hours, first accessing the wiki at 74 hours. But I would expect a first playthrough for most people to be around 18-30 hours.
